- Had a driving lesson with Steve at Atlas Driving. Here's what we covered: 1. How far way should we be from the curb? 30 cm. How to adjust the different car settings. Seat: First pump it up to desired height using the control on the left hand side. Chin should be well above the steering wheel. Then you can adjust the back and forth using the lever under the front of the seat. 
- Then adjust the mirrors
- Adjust the steering wheel by pulling the lever on the bottom left and moving it
- His car has an auto handbreak feature. You can take off the auto handbreak feature by pushing it down, then turn it back on by pulling it up. 
- When pulling out of a parking, you put yourself in drive mode, then scan all around from the right shoulder check, right mirror check, in front of you, left mirror check, left shoulder check. 
- When changing lanes always do a mirror check then a shoulder check
- When turning do a shoulder check before turning.
- It's more efficient and okay to cross hands when turning
- When driving, stick to the line on your left hand side. From your pov it should look like the lines are coming into the car on the front left panel and maybe even slightly to the right. 
- When stopping, you can use the mirror as a guide, line should be just under the left mirror. 
- When turning, always turn into the lane that's closest.

- Learnt to parallel park. 1. Indicate. 2. Drive next to the car you're trying to park behind (till you can see the back of their car on your back window mid-line), leave about 3 inches of space. 3. Switch to reverse and, do a full scan from left to right. 4. Lock seering wheel to the right and drive in reverse till you are about 45 degree angle from the other car. 5. Straighten wheel and keep reversing till the red line of the camera touches the top of the pavement (meanwhile repeatedly looking out the back, right mirror and camera). 6. Then lock hard left and keep going till you are parallel with the pavement. 7. Check slope of car and lock wheels accordingly.
